behaviors.description: Review input is scoped to changed lines only, not full file content
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is diff parsing bugs in edge cases (binary files, renames, deletions, very large diffs). A pure Unit test with mocked diff parsing would miss real parsing edge cases in actual git diff format. Need to generate real diffs from actual git operations to verify scoping behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a local git repository with multi-file changes. Generate diff output using real git commands. Verify the scoping logic correctly identifies only the changed line ranges, not the entire file content. Test edge cases: deleted lines, renamed files, binary file markers.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are positioned correctly in the PR diff view
behaviors.justification: GitHub's PR diff positioning has specific format requirements (diff line numbers vs file line numbers, 0-based vs 1-based indexing, left/right side markers). A mock validation might accept formats that real GitHub rejects. Real GitHub API is the actual requirement — the failure only manifests when posting to actual GitHub and seeing the comment in wrong position or API rejection.
behaviors.test_description: Post actual inline comments to a real GitHub pull request at calculated line positions. Verify comments appear at intended code locations in GitHub UI. Test multi-file scenarios and edge positions (first/last diff line). Verify API response indicates successful positioning.

behaviors.description: Agent identifies at least one real defect in code containing a known introduced bug
behaviors.justification: The core behavior is LLM code analysis capability. A mocked or stubbed analyzer would trivially pass by returning 'found bug'. Real failure: the actual LLM model fails to identify subtle defects. This depends on LLM non-determinism and reasoning quality — outcomes differ across model versions and runs.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a code diff with a deliberately introduced defect (null pointer dereference, missing error check, logic bug). Run real LLM analysis on the code. Assert that the LLM-generated findings list includes at least one finding that correctly identifies the introduced bug. Run 3+ times to verify consistency.

behaviors.description: Every finding in summary has a matching inline comment; no orphaned findings
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is async coordination or filtering logic bugs. Unit tests of the deduplication logic might pass, but real failures happen when posting comments asynchronously: inline comments fail to post while summary succeeds (orphaning findings), or race conditions in tracking. Need to test actual async posting behavior, not just logic.
behaviors.test_description: Configure mock GitHub API to accept inline and summary comment requests. Generate findings list from code analysis. Post inline comments asynchronously, then post summary. Verify every finding in summary has a corresponding inline comment in the mock API call history. Test with delayed/failing inline comment posts to catch race conditions.

behaviors.description: Full sequence completes end-to-end without manual intervention on real PR
behaviors.justification: The system requires multiple coordinated steps with real dependencies: (1) parse real diff (Integration-level), (2) LLM analyze code (Agentic-level), (3) post inline comments to real GitHub (System-level), (4) post summary (System-level). Failures only emerge in the full pipeline: LLM output format unexpected by poster, sequencing issues (summary before inlines finish), or timing-dependent GitHub API errors. Unit/Integration/System tests of individual steps would each pass but miss inter-step failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real pull request on GitHub with intentional bugs. Run the full review automation: fetch diff, invoke LLM analysis, post inline review comments, post summary comment. Verify end-to-end completion without failures. Verify summary and all inline comments appear in correct positions in the actual PR. Test recovery from transient GitHub API failures.
